title: denyWrite:[~] は allowWrite 例外も cwd 暗黙許可も両方潰す (アンチパターン) -> 全プローブ allow x ng

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > inside.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > $HOME/lab-fs-write/probe.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > $HOME/lab-glob-XYZ/probe.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

notes: 1-variable diff from b: + denyWrite:[~]. ANTIPATTERN. deny wins over allow in the sandbox FS layer, and cwd (the repo) is under ~, so denyWrite:[~] removes BOTH the allowWrite:[~/lab-fs-write] exception (w-allowlisted b->d ok->ng) AND the implicit cwd write (w-cwd a/b ok -> d ng). This addresses S2 GAPS G8: both halves of the pitfall (cwd loss + allowlist loss) are now measured, not extrapolated. Every cell is allow x ng. Lesson: never denyWrite:[~]; scope writes with allowWrite only.
hypothesis: denyWrite:[~] does not merely cancel the allowWrite exception; because cwd is under ~, it also strips the implicit cwd write. Both w-cwd and w-allowlisted become allow x ng -- you lose the ability to write your own working directory.
